algorithms.title: The DISASTER Paradigm — Response Checklist
algorithms.description: The eight ordered components applied by the first-arriving and every subsequent responder at any all-hazards event.
steps: Detection: size up — do needs exceed resources? Report casualties, hazards, and needs; declare an MCI/disaster if so.
Incident command: the first trained responder establishes and announces command; transfer as senior officers arrive.
Safety and security: protect in order — self, team, public, patients; PPE, staging, perimeter, resource security.
Assess hazards: search for ongoing and secondary hazards (fire, collapse, hazmat, devices); reassess continuously.
Support: request staff, supplies, transport, and special teams early — anticipate the incident an hour ahead.
Triage and treatment: apply SALT; deliver the greatest good for the greatest number.
Evacuation: move the right patient to the right destination; distribute across receiving facilities.
Recovery: begin during response — restore systems, support people, capture lessons learned.
